rulings.Case Title: ATLANTIC CREDIT AND FINANCE INC VS. ALLAN NG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtns To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions; Dec; Memo Of P & A And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 30, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ATLANTIC CREDIT AND FINANCE INCORPORATED'S MOTION To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Trent Thornley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: JEAN-PIERRE IVAN VS. TRADER JOE'S COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2012, Line 1, counsel for PLAINTIFF JEAN-PIERRE IVAN's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Appearances required. The moving papers suggest the "next" hearing is set on July 3, 2012, which must be a typographical error. Among other things, this means that it is unclear if granting the motion now would be prejudicial to the client. The record is unclear that the client has been served or that the address provided is current. If the Court's concerns on these matters are addressed at the hearing the motion to withdraw may be granted. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: BERNADETTE MCMENAMIN VS. NORTHERN TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, A FEDERALLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Bernadette Mcmenamin'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Northern Trust, Na To Further Respond To Demand For Production Of Documents, Set Three; And To Produce All Documents And Things Responsive Thereto
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2012, Line 22, Plaintiff Bernadette Mcmenamin'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Northern Trust, Na To Further Respond To Demand For Production Of Documents, Set Three; And To Produce All Documents And Things Responsive Thereto. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Halperin. In summary, that recommendation provides as follows: Granted, in part, as to Request for Production no. 1 (the investigative file concerning the harassment claim in the San Francisco office), and any descriptions of Ms. Chase's duties that may be found in her employment file. Denied as to all other Requests for Production and sanctions. For Request for Production no. 1, the responding parties are ordered to produce redacted copies where the claimant's and investigation target's names redacted and production to occur pursuant to a protective order. For Ms. Chase's file, descriptions of Ms. Chase's duties that may be found in her employment file are directly relevant to Plaintiff's claims that Plaintiff's termination pursuant to the reduction in force was a pretext. However, production of other materials in the employment file are not be justified. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling, appending a copy of the Pro Tem's recommendations, and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: FOREMAN & BRASSO, ATTORNEYS AT LAW VS. JOSEPH ALVARADO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discharge From Liability As A Mere Stakeholder
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2012, Line 11, PLAINTIFF FOREMAN & BRASSO'S Motion For Discharge From Liability As A Mere Stakeholder. Granted. Plaintiff has established that it has no interest in the money deposited with the Court and dismissal is proper under CCP § 386.5. Plaintiff is awarded $2750 in attorney's fees pursuant to CCP § 386.6, which sum is payable out of the funds on deposit with the Court. The request for costs is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff failed to file a memorandum of costs or sufficiently explain what costs it seeks to recover. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM VEDERMAN MD VS. LAW OFFICE OF STEVEN D. HILYARD APC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-507391 WITH CASE CGC-11-516181
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2012, Line 14, PLAINTIFF WILLIAM VEDERMAN'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-507391 WITH CASE CGC-11-516181. The motion to consolidate is granted. However, this order does not extend the time in which discovery of any kind must be had on the complaint in 11-507391 nor does it effect the right (or not) to a jury trial in that case. This order does not affect the trial date in any case, and accordingly if a party desires to continue the trial in 11-507391, a separate application to the Department of the Presiding Judge must be made. Assuming a single trial of the consolidated cases, the trial judge will determine the order of proof. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M SAGONOWSKY VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re Ntc Demurrer To Pltfs 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT MARX & DUFFY, LLP & RICHARD MARX'S Demurrer To PLAINTIFF'S 1st Amended Complaint. Overruled. Whether Sagonowsky's claims are barred by the statute of limitations is a question of fact that is not the subject of a demurrer. The adequacy of the evidence (see e.g., Amended Reply at 10 line 12) is not generally passed on at demurer. The parties' requests for judicial notice are unopposed and are accordingly granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRIAN CARIDEO VS. STANLEY KELLEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2012, Line 18, DEFENDANTS STANLEY KELLEY, ANTHONY KELLEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION. The Requests for Judicial Notice are granted as unopposed. Sustained without leave. Kelley Kennedy (KK) has filed a complaint in intervention, alleging elder abuse against the decedent. Defendants S. Kelley and A. Kelley note that the complaint does not comply with requirements of CCP § 377.32. See generally, Lickter v. Lickter, 189 Cal.App.4th 712, 722 (2010). KK agrees the statute has not been complied with. The statutory requirement is that of a declaration that no proceeding for the administration of the estate be pending; and there is such a proceeding. No personal representative has yet been appointed, and KK suggests the Probate Court may not appoint one in the near future. This combination of factors frustrates the attempts to proceed with the elder abuse action, as no-one appears to have standing to proceed with that action. The only precedent directly on point suggests that I sustain the demurrer. Exarhos v. Exarhos, 159 Cal.Ap..4th 898 (2008)(reporting but not deciding decision of trial court); Anderson v. Hickman, 2008 U.S.Dist.LEXIS 49130 (No 07-1466 LKK June 11, 2008). Cases proposed by KK do not address the impact of the statute, Olson v. Toy, 46 Cal.App.4th 818 (1996), although as KK rightly notes there is general precedent that standing issues ought to be resolved in favor prosecuting elder abuse cases. Estate of Lowrie, 118 Cal.App.4th 220 (2004). The central problem is that the statute is unequivocal in its wording, and I do not see how I am permitted to avoid it. One remedy may be to ask the Probate Court appoint a personal representative, or otherwise cause that proceeding to no longer exist for purposes of CCP § 377.32. If KK anticipates such a proceeding, it may be appropriate to sustain with leave to amend, or to overrule the demurrer without prejudice to a plea in abatement. The parties are advised of William F. Rylaarsdam, et al., CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 2:31.7 (Rutter Group, 2012), citing Parsons v. Tickner, 31 Cal.App.4th 1513, 1524 (1995)(remedy is plea in abatement). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MYERS VS. JENNY PEI LIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Appointing Receiver And For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2012, Line 21, PLAINTIFF FRANK MYERS' Amended Motion For Reconsideration Appointing Receiver And For Preliminary Injunction. Continued to August 7, 2012 per the Second Amended Notice. Although the Court received courtesy copies of the Second Amended Notice, with attachments, it does not appear that the Second Amended Notice was filed. Plaintiff is instructed to file the Second Amended Notice, along with the attachments, prior to the August 7 hearing date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/CK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK MYERS VS. JENNY PEI LIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 30, 2012, Line 21, DEFENDANTS JENNY LIN, FRANCISCAN HOTEL, LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Denied. By generally appearing in this action, Defendants waived any arguments regarding defective service. See CCP § 410.50 ("A general appearance by a party is equivalent to personal service of summons on such party."). See also, Factor Health Management, LLC v. Superior Court, 132 Cal.App.4th 246, 251 (2005)("a motion for a preliminary injunction "involve[s] a determination related to the merits of the case¿."); 366-386 Geary St., L.P. v. Superior Court, 219 Cal.App.3d 1186, 1194 (1990); Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Sparks Const., Inc., 114 Cal. App. 4th 1135, 1145 (2004). Defendants litigated a motion for preliminary injunction as well as other motions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/CK)
